description: Phase 1 of 2 — DDGC Reactive Hooks And Skill Usage Limits. Close the highest-leverage open game-gap blockers first by building deterministic reactive combat plumbing, implementing riposte and guard semantics, and enforcing LimitPerTurn / LimitPerBattle through game-layer state.

userStories.title: P1-0: Inventory reactive and limited-use touchpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a precise inventory of the migrated skills, statuses, and encounters that depend on riposte, guard, or usage limits so Phase 1 implementation is driven by real DDGC touchpoints instead of generic guesses.
acceptanceCriteria: A checked-in note or section in progress/logging docs lists the migrated DDGC units or skills that currently rely on riposte, guard, per-turn limits, or per-battle limits
The inventory distinguishes already-migrated content from future content that can wait for later phases
At least one concrete reactive case and one concrete usage-limit case are selected as implementation anchors for later stories
userStories.notes: Discovery slice. Keep output lightweight and implementation-focused.

userStories.title: P1-1: Add reactive combat event types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need explicit reactive-event data structures so post-hit reactions are represented as first-class game-layer events rather than implicit side effects hidden inside damage resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-layer combat code exposes explicit event or record types for reactive follow-up work
The event model can represent who was hit, who may react, and what triggered the reaction
A focused test proves the event structure can be created deterministically from a resolved damage step
userStories.notes: Data-model slice only. Do not execute reactions yet.

userStories.title: P1-2: Add deterministic reactive queue harness
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a deterministic queue or ordering harness for reactive follow-ups so multiple reaction candidates can be evaluated in one stable sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated game-layer helper or module exists for scheduling reactive follow-up actions
When multiple reactions are possible, evaluation order is deterministic and documented in tests
A focused test proves queued reactions do not re-apply the original hit or recurse indefinitely
userStories.notes: Plumbing slice. This is the execution backbone for riposte and guard.

userStories.title: P1-3: Trace reactive events explicitly
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reactive events to appear in battle traces so later semantic review can distinguish the base attack from the follow-up action it caused.
acceptanceCriteria: Reactive follow-up actions produce explicit trace entries instead of blending into the original attack output
Trace output identifies the trigger relationship between the original hit and the reaction
A focused test proves trace serialization is stable when a reactive event is present
userStories.notes: Observability slice before full mechanic enablement.

userStories.title: P1-4: Detect riposte-ready actors
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the reactive harness to recognize riposte marker statuses on the correct actor set so the game knows when a counter-attack is eligible.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-layer combat code can detect riposte markers from the active combat state without framework changes
The detection logic only marks valid riposte candidates for qualifying incoming hits
A focused test proves non-riposte actors are ignored while riposte-marked actors are surfaced once
userStories.notes: Eligibility slice only. No counter-attack execution yet.

userStories.title: P1-5: Execute riposte counter-attacks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need riposte-eligible actors to execute a single deterministic counter-attack through the reactive harness so migrated DDGC riposte units stop behaving like passive status carriers.
acceptanceCriteria: A riposte-marked actor can issue exactly one reactive counter-attack per qualifying hit
At least one migrated DDGC family or encounter fixture proves riposte behavior end-to-end
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md and SEMANTIC_GAPS.md are updated if riposte support meaningfully closes part of B-008

userStories.title: P1-6: Detect guard protection relationships
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the combat layer to resolve which ally is guarding which target so incoming attacks can be redirected intentionally instead of merely tagged.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-layer combat state can resolve a protected target and its guarding actor deterministically
Detection works from existing status or content data without requiring framework-crate changes
A focused test proves the correct guard relationship is selected when a protected target is attacked

userStories.title: P1-7: Redirect guarded damage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need guarded hits to land on the guarding ally instead of the protected actor so defensive DDGC behavior preserves actual battlefield protection semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: A guarded target no longer receives the original damage directly when redirection succeeds
The guarding ally receives the redirected hit through one deterministic pathway
A focused test proves guard redirection coexists cleanly with the reactive queue and trace output

userStories.title: P1-8: Add reactive golden trace coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a committed reactive trace fixture so future refactors cannot silently reorder or remove riposte and guard follow-up behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed fixture under fixtures/semantic_battles or fixtures/encounters captures at least one reactive combat sequence
A regression test compares live output against the committed reactive trace
The trace clearly separates original attacks, redirected hits, and counter-attacks for review
userStories.notes: Locks Phase 1 reactive behavior before moving on.

userStories.title: P1-9: Add skill usage counter state
userStories.description: As a developer, I need dedicated per-skill counter state for turn scope and battle scope so usage-limit enforcement has a concrete runtime model.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-layer combat state tracks usage counts by actor and skill for both turn and battle scopes
The counters are isolated from cooldown logic rather than overloading cooldown fields
A focused test proves unrelated skills do not affect each other's counters

userStories.title: P1-10: Reset counters at turn boundaries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need turn-scoped counters to reset at the right time so per-turn limits behave consistently across actor turns and round transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Turn-scoped usage counters reset when the relevant actor turn boundary advances
Battle-scoped counters do not reset during the same transition
A focused test proves the reset boundary is deterministic and not tied to unrelated combat events

userStories.title: P1-11: Reset counters at battle boundaries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need encounter-scoped counters to reset only when a new battle starts so per-battle limits survive cooldown refreshes but not new encounters.
acceptanceCriteria: Battle-scoped counters persist for the full encounter lifetime
Starting a fresh encounter resets battle-scoped counts cleanly
A focused test proves battle reset behavior without depending on manual state cleanup
userStories.notes: Completes the B-005 state lifecycle model.

userStories.title: P1-12: Enforce per-turn usage limits
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the game layer to reject or skip commands that exceed per-turn limits so DDGC skills with turn-bounded usage stop repeating freely.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one migrated or fixture-backed skill can declare a per-turn usage limit in DDGC game-layer metadata
Command resolution blocks or skips only the over-limit skill use while leaving legal commands intact
A deterministic test proves the same skill becomes available again after the correct turn reset

userStories.title: P1-13: Enforce per-battle usage limits
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the game layer to enforce encounter-wide limits so once-per-battle DDGC skills preserve their intended rarity even if cooldowns would otherwise allow reuse.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one migrated or fixture-backed skill can declare a per-battle limit in DDGC game-layer metadata
Over-limit battle-scope usage is rejected or skipped deterministically within the same encounter
A deterministic test proves a new encounter restores availability while the current encounter does not

userStories.title: P1-14: Add regression coverage for reactive hooks and usage limits
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Phase 1 mechanics locked by regression tests so later conditional or hit-policy work cannot silently break the newly closed semantic gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Regression tests cover riposte, guard redirection, per-turn limits, and per-battle limits
The test suite uses game_ddgc_headless public APIs rather than framework internals
cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml exits 0 with the full Phase 1 coverage enabled
userStories.notes: Phase 1 closeout story. Start Phase 2 only after this passes.
